location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in stock plan administration
Base pay range $132,000 - $165,000 in San Francisco area

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience in stock plan administration, including with public companies on a global scale.
must_haves.qualifier: including with public companies on a global scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have experience effectively partnering with Total Rewards teams, with international equity plans (including M&A equity integration), and supporting Compensation Committee processes.
You operate like an owner. You are self-motivated, reliable, flexible, and can drive progress under tight deadlines.

You have exceptional analytical and conceptual thinking skills, have a strong track record of achievement and have a strong tendency toward cross-functional relationship building and a bias toward action.
You can distill complex concepts into simple, easy to understand communications.
Very strong Excel skills are required.
Deep knowledge of equity accounting and operating in international jurisdictions.

Support administration of Lyft's global equity programs including processing all awards, grants, exercises, cancellations and the issuance of all new grants in the US and in foreign jurisdictions, including managing RSUs, PSUs, ESPP, capital markets transactions and other equity programs.
Support all aspects of SOX compliance and continuously work to build efficiencies around execution and documentation of key controls.
Support the timely filing of Section 16 reports (Forms 3, 4 and 5) with the SEC.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of Workday, ADP, and the Schwab Equiview platform is a huge plus.
You have a strong desire to learn and teach, constantly seeking opportunities to develop team members, and be developed by team members.
Sense of humor, positive attitude, low ego, and a willingness to dive into new areas.

scope.surface: Global equity programs including RSUs, PSUs, ESPP, capital markets transactions, M&A equity integration, Section 16 filings, and employee equity education
primary_metrics_inferred: Accuracy and timeliness of equity award processing
International equity program coverage and compliance
Employee education program reach and effectiveness
core_responsibilities: Support administration of Lyft's global equity programs including processing all awards, grants, exercises, cancellations and the issuance of all new grants in the US and in foreign jurisdictions
Continue to drive system automation on the Schwab Equiview platform across Workday and ADP
Develop and deliver world class employee education programs for new and continuing employees
Support all aspects of SOX compliance and continuously work to build efficiencies around execution and documentation of key controls
Engage with senior leadership from the legal, finance, and people organizations to design future equity programs and policies
Coordinate closely with the payroll team to ensure proper withholding of tax from equity proceeds
Define domestic and international integration strategies (deal specific) for equity, support M&A equity integration process
Assist with D&O Questionnaires and Proxy disclosure regarding director and executive officer stock ownership
Support the 10b5-1 program for executives and insiders across two brokers
Support the timely filing of Section 16 reports (Forms 3, 4 and 5) with the SEC

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned as separate from base pay

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($132,000 - $165,000 base in SF area)
Clear and detailed list of responsibilities with no vague platitudes
Reports directly to Director, Global Equity Programs — clear organizational positioning
Hybrid schedule explicitly defined (3 days: Mon, Wed, Thu) with up to 4 weeks fully remote per year
Role involves high-visibility work (Section 16, Proxy, Compensation Committee, M&A integration)
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear if available
Hybrid requires in-office 3 days/week in San Francisco — eliminates non-local candidates
Salary range is San Francisco-specific; other location ranges not disclosed

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and base comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — no direct reports mentioned in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from Lyft being a public B2C ride-sharing company)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: CEP certification is listed in the same sentence as Excel ('Very strong Excel skills are required. Knowledge of Workday, ADP, and the Schwab Equiview platform is a huge plus. CEP certification is preferred.') — Excel is explicitly required (hard), CEP is preferred (soft), but Workday/ADP/Schwab Equiview are described as 'a huge plus' (soft). All classified accordingly.

The JD does not specify whether this role is San Francisco only or if other locations are considered — the comp range references 'San Francisco area' and recruiter is noted to share location-specific ranges, suggesting other locations may be possible but are not confirmed.
